```js
hs.reload()
```

methods.name: runningApplications
methods.rawDocumentation:
Fetch all running applications
- Returns: An array of all currently running applications
- Example:
```js
const apps = hs.application.runningApplications()
apps.forEach(a => console.log(a.title))
```
methods.description: Fetch all running applications

returns.description: An array of all currently running applications

methods.name: matchingName
methods.rawDocumentation:
Fetch the first running application that matches a name
- Parameter name: The applicaiton name to search for
- Returns: The first matching application, or nil if none matched
- Example:
```js
const safari = hs.application.matchingName("Safari")
```
methods.description: Fetch the first running application that matches a name

params.name: name
params.description: The applicaiton name to search for

returns.description: The first matching application, or nil if none matched

methods.description: Fetch the first running application that matches a Bundle ID

params.name: bundleID

returns.description: The first matching application, or nil if none matched

methods.rawDocumentation:
Fetch the filesystem path for an application

```js

```

params.name: bundleID
params.description: The application bundle identifier to search for (e.g. "com.apple.Safari")

methods.name: iconForBundleID
methods.rawDocumentation:
Render the application's icon as a base64-encoded PNG string. Use the
returned string as the body of a `data:image/png;base64,…` URL to render
the icon in HTML/SwiftUI without exposing the underlying .icns path.
Falls back to NSWorkspace's generic icon if no application is found.
- Parameter bundleID: The application bundle identifier (e.g. "com.apple.Safari")
- Returns: The base64-encoded PNG bytes, or null if the bundle could not be located
- Example:
```js
const b64 = hs.application.iconForBundleID("com.apple.Safari")
element.src = "data:image/png;base64," + b64
```
methods.description:
Render the application's icon as a base64-encoded PNG string. Use the
returned string as the body of a `data:image/png;base64,…` URL to render
the icon in HTML/SwiftUI without exposing the underlying .icns path.
Falls back to NSWorkspace's generic icon if no application is found.

params.name: bundleID
params.description: The application bundle identifier (e.g. "com.apple.Safari")

returns.description: The base64-encoded PNG bytes, or null if the bundle could not be located

params.name: bundleID
params.description: The application bundle identifier to search for (e.g. "com.apple.Safari")

returns.description: An array of strings containing any filesystem paths that were found

methods.name: pathForFileType
methods.rawDocumentation:
Fetch filesystem path for an application able to open a given file type
- Parameter fileType: The file type to search for. This can be a UTType identifier, a MIME type, or a filename extension
- Returns: The path to an application for the given filetype, or il if none were found
- Example:
```js

```
methods.description: Fetch filesystem path for an application able to open a given file type

params.name: fileType
params.description: The file type to search for. This can be a UTType identifier, a MIME type, or a filename extension

returns.description: The path to an application for the given filetype, or il if none were found

methods.name: pathsForFileType
methods.rawDocumentation:
Fetch filesystem paths for applications able to open a given file type
- Parameter fileType: The file type to search for. This can be a UTType identifier, a MIME type, or a filename extension
- Returns: An array of strings containing the filesystem paths for any applications that were found
- Example:

methods.description: Fetch filesystem paths for applications able to open a given file type

params.name: fileType
params.description: The file type to search for. This can be a UTType identifier, a MIME type, or a filename extension

returns.description: An array of strings containing the filesystem paths for any applications that were found

methods.name: launchOrFocus
methods.rawDocumentation:
Launch an application, or give it focus if it's already running
- Parameter bundleID: A bundle identifier for the app to launch/focus (e.g. "com.apple.Safari")
- Returns: {Promise<boolean>} A Promise that resolves to true if successful, false otherwise


params.description: A bundle identifier for the app to launch/focus (e.g. "com.apple.Safari")

returns.description: A Promise that resolves to true if successful, false otherwise

methods.name: installedApps
methods.rawDocumentation:
Enumerate every `.app` bundle under the standard application roots,
plus any caller-supplied extra roots. Results are cached for 30 seconds
per unique `extraRoots` argument; the cache is dropped automatically
when the contents of any scanned root change (an app is installed or
deleted), so changes are visible on the next call. Call
`invalidateInstalledAppsCache()` to force a rescan by hand.

Roots scanned in order (dedup by bundleID — first wins):
  1. /Applications
  2. ~/Applications
  3. /System/Applications
  4. /System/Applications/Utilities
  5. /System/Cryptexes/App/System/Applications (Safari — its
     /Applications symlink is flagged hidden, so it must be scanned
     at its real cryptex location)
  6. /System/Library/CoreServices/Applications (Keychain Access,
     Archive Utility, Directory Utility, …)
  7. /System/Library/CoreServices/Finder.app (Finder)
  8. Any caller-supplied extra roots

A root may be a single `.app` bundle (like the Finder entry above), in
which case that bundle itself is the result — extra roots may use this
form too.

Both bundle layouts are understood: regular macOS bundles
(`Contents/Info.plist`) and the wrapper layout the App Store uses for
iPhone/iPad apps on Apple silicon (`Foo.app/Wrapper/<Inner>.app/`).
Wrapper apps report the inner bundle's metadata (that's where the
localized `displayName` lives) with `path` pointing at the outer
`.app` — the thing you launch or reveal in Finder.

Bundles with `LSBackgroundOnly = true` (true daemons with no UI) are
skipped. Menu-bar-only apps (`LSUIElement = true`, e.g. Hammerspoon 1,
Bartender, ClipMenu) are included because users still launch them.
`iconPath`, when non-null, points at the bundle's primary icon on disk
(`.icns` for macOS bundles, the app-icon `.png` for iOS wrapper apps).

- Parameter extraRoots: Optional array of additional directories to scan.
- Returns: Array of `{name, displayName, bundleID, path, iconPath, version}`
- Example:
```js
const apps = hs.application.installedApps()
console.log(apps[0].displayName, apps[0].bundleID)

// With extra roots:
const more = hs.application.installedApps(['~/Tools', '~/Dev/Apps'])
```
methods.description:
Enumerate every `.app` bundle under the standard application roots,
plus any caller-supplied extra roots. Results are cached for 30 seconds
per unique `extraRoots` argument; the cache is dropped automatically
when the contents of any scanned root change (an app is installed or
deleted), so changes are visible on the next call. Call
`invalidateInstalledAppsCache()` to force a rescan by hand.
1. /Applications
2. ~/Applications
3. /System/Applications
4. /System/Applications/Utilities
5. /System/Cryptexes/App/System/Applications (Safari — its
/Applications symlink is flagged hidden, so it must be scanned
at its real cryptex location)
6. /System/Library/CoreServices/Applications (Keychain Access,
Archive Utility, Directory Utility, …)
7. /System/Library/CoreServices/Finder.app (Finder)
8. Any caller-supplied extra roots
A root may be a single `.app` bundle (like the Finder entry above), in
which case that bundle itself is the result — extra roots may use this
form too.
Both bundle layouts are understood: regular macOS bundles
(`Contents/Info.plist`) and the wrapper layout the App Store uses for
iPhone/iPad apps on Apple silicon (`Foo.app/Wrapper/<Inner>.app/`).
Wrapper apps report the inner bundle's metadata (that's where the
localized `displayName` lives) with `path` pointing at the outer
`.app` — the thing you launch or reveal in Finder.
Bundles with `LSBackgroundOnly = true` (true daemons with no UI) are
skipped. Menu-bar-only apps (`LSUIElement = true`, e.g. Hammerspoon 1,
Bartender, ClipMenu) are included because users still launch them.
`iconPath`, when non-null, points at the bundle's primary icon on disk
(`.icns` for macOS bundles, the app-icon `.png` for iOS wrapper apps).

params.name: extraRoots
params.description: Optional array of additional directories to scan.

returns.description: Array of `{name, displayName, bundleID, path, iconPath, version}`

methods.name: invalidateInstalledAppsCache
methods.rawDocumentation:
Force the next call to `installedApps()` to rescan from disk.
- Example:
```js
hs.application.invalidateInstalledAppsCache()
```
methods.description: Force the next call to `installedApps()` to rescan from disk.

methods.name: killPid
methods.signature: func killPid(_ pid: Int, _ force: Bool) -> Bool
methods.rawDocumentation:
Send SIGTERM (force=false) or SIGKILL (force=true) to an arbitrary PID.
Refuses to signal PID 0, 1, or this process. Returns true if the signal
was delivered, false on error (logged via AKError).
- Parameter pid: Target PID
- Parameter force: When true sends SIGKILL; otherwise SIGTERM.
- Returns: true on success
- Example:
```js
hs.application.killPid(12345, false)   // graceful
hs.application.killPid(12345, true)    // force
```
methods.description:
Send SIGTERM (force=false) or SIGKILL (force=true) to an arbitrary PID.
Refuses to signal PID 0, 1, or this process. Returns true if the signal
was delivered, false on error (logged via AKError).

params.name: pid
params.description: Target PID

params.name: force
params.description: When true sends SIGKILL; otherwise SIGTERM.

examples.code:
hs.application.killPid(12345, false)   // graceful
hs.application.killPid(12345, true)    // force

methods.name: addWatcher
methods.rawDocumentation:
Create a watcher for application events
- Parameters:
   - listener: {(event: string, app: HSApplication | null) => void} A javascript function/lambda to call when any application event is received. The function will be called with two parameters: the name of the event, and the associated HSApplication object
- Example:
```js
hs.application.addWatcher((event, app) => {
    console.log(event + app && app.title)
})
```
methods.description: Create a watcher for application events

params.name: listener
params.description: A javascript function/lambda to call when any application event is received. The function will be called with two parameters: the name of the event, and the associated HSApplication object

methods.name: removeWatcher
methods.rawDocumentation:
Remove a watcher for application events
- Parameters:
  - listener: The javascript function/lambda that was previously being used to handle events
- Example:
```js
hs.application.removeWatcher(myHandler)
```
methods.description: Remove a watcher for application events

params.name: listener
params.description: The javascript function/lambda that was previously being used to handle events

types.name: HSApplicationAPI
types.rawDocumentation: Object representing an application. You should not instantiate this directly in JavaScript, but rather, use the methods from hs.application which will return appropriate HSApplication objects.
types.description: Object representing an application. You should not instantiate this directly in JavaScript, but rather, use the methods from hs.application which will return appropriate HSApplication objects.

methods.rawDocumentation:
Terminate the application
- Returns: True if the application was terminated, otherwise false
- Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.matchingName("Calculator")
app.kill()
```
methods.description: Terminate the application

returns.description: True if the application was terminated, otherwise false

methods.rawDocumentation:
Force-terminate the application

```js
app.kill9()
```
methods.description: Force-terminate the application

returns.description: True if the application was force-terminated, otherwise false

methods.rawDocumentation:
The application's HSAXElement object, for use with the hs.ax APIs
- Returns: An HSAXElement object, or nil if it could not be obtained
- Example:

const ax = app.axElement()

methods.name: activate
methods.rawDocumentation:
Bring this application to the foreground
- Parameter allWindows?: Pass true to raise all application windows. Defaults to false.
- Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.matchingName("Safari")
app.activate()
app.activate(true)
```
methods.description: Bring this application to the foreground

params.name: allWindows
params.description: Pass true to raise all application windows. Defaults to false.

methods.name: hide
methods.rawDocumentation:
Hide this application and all its windows
- Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.matchingName("Safari")
app.hide()
```
methods.description: Hide this application and all its windows

methods.name: unhide
methods.rawDocumentation:
Unhide this application
- Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.matchingName("Safari")
app.unhide()
```
methods.description: Unhide this application

methods.name: getMenuItems
methods.rawDocumentation:
Get the full menu structure of this application
- Note: This traverses the accessibility hierarchy and may be slow for apps with large menus.
- Returns: An array of top-level menu objects, each with title and items keys, or null if unavailable
- Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.frontmost()
app.getMenuItems().forEach(m => console.log(m.title))
```
methods.description: Get the full menu structure of this application

returns.description: An array of top-level menu objects, each with title and items keys, or null if unavailable

notes: This traverses the accessibility hierarchy and may be slow for apps with large menus.

methods.name: findMenuItemByName
methods.rawDocumentation:
Find a menu item by searching all menus for a matching title (case-insensitive)
- Parameter name: The menu item title to search for
- Returns: An object with title and enabled keys, or null if not found
- Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.frontmost()
const item = app.findMenuItemByName("Select All")
console.log(item.enabled)
```
methods.description: Find a menu item by searching all menus for a matching title (case-insensitive)

params.name: name
params.description: The menu item title to search for

returns.description: An object with title and enabled keys, or null if not found

methods.name: findMenuItemByPath
methods.rawDocumentation:
Find a menu item by following a hierarchical path of titles
- Parameter path: An array of menu titles forming a path from the top-level menu to the item, e.g. ["Edit", "Select All"]
- Returns: An object with title and enabled keys, or null if not found
- Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.frontmost()
const item = app.findMenuItemByPath(["Edit", "Select All"])
console.log(item.enabled)
```
methods.description: Find a menu item by following a hierarchical path of titles

params.name: path
params.description: An array of menu titles forming a path from the top-level menu to the item, e.g. ["Edit", "Select All"]

returns.description: An object with title and enabled keys, or null if not found

methods.name: selectMenuItemByName
methods.rawDocumentation:
Click a menu item found by searching all menus for a matching title (case-insensitive)
- Parameter name: The menu item title to search for
- Returns: true if the menu item was found and clicked, false otherwise
- Example:
```js
const safari = hs.application.matchingName("Safari")
safari.selectMenuItemByName("New Window")
```
methods.description: Click a menu item found by searching all menus for a matching title (case-insensitive)

params.name: name
params.description: The menu item title to search for

returns.description: true if the menu item was found and clicked, false otherwise

methods.name: selectMenuItemByPath
methods.rawDocumentation:
Click a menu item found by following a hierarchical path of titles
- Parameter path: An array of menu titles forming a path from the top-level menu to the item, e.g. ["File", "New Window"]
- Returns: true if the menu item was found and clicked, false otherwise
- Example:
```js
const safari = hs.application.matchingName("Safari")
safari.selectMenuItemByPath(["File", "New Window"])
```
methods.description: Click a menu item found by following a hierarchical path of titles

params.name: path
params.description: An array of menu titles forming a path from the top-level menu to the item, e.g. ["File", "New Window"]

returns.description: true if the menu item was found and clicked, false otherwise

methods.name: findWindow
methods.rawDocumentation:
Find windows whose title contains the given string (case-insensitive)
- Parameter pattern: A string to search for in window titles
- Returns: An array of matching HSWindow objects
- Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.frontmost()
app.findWindow("untitled").forEach(w => console.log(w.title))
```
methods.description: Find windows whose title contains the given string (case-insensitive)

params.name: pattern
params.description: A string to search for in window titles

returns.description: An array of matching HSWindow objects

methods.name: getWindow
methods.rawDocumentation:
Get the first window with exactly the given title
- Parameter title: The exact window title to search for
- Returns: The matching HSWindow, or null if not found
- Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.frontmost()
const win = app.getWindow("index.html")
```
methods.description: Get the first window with exactly the given title

params.name: title
params.description: The exact window title to search for

returns.description: The matching HSWindow, or null if not found

examples.code:
const app = hs.application.frontmost()
const win = app.getWindow("index.html")

properties.name: isRunning
properties.rawDocumentation:
Whether the application process is still running
- Returns: true if the application is running, false if it has terminated
- Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.matchingName("Calculator")
app.kill()
console.log(app.isRunning)
```
properties.description: Whether the application process is still running

properties.name: kind
properties.rawDocumentation:
The kind of application: "standard" (regular dock app), "accessory" (no dock), or "background" (agent)
- Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.frontmost()
console.log(app.kind)
```
properties.description: The kind of application: "standard" (regular dock app), "accessory" (no dock), or "background" (agent)

methods.name: addWatcher
methods.rawDocumentation:
Register a listener for all system-level audio configuration events.

The listener receives one of the following event name strings:
- `"dOut"` — the default output device changed
- `"dIn"` — the default input device changed
- `"dSErr"` — the default alert sound device changed
- `"dev+"` — an audio device was added
- `"dev-"` — an audio device was removed

- Parameter listener: {(event: string) => void} A JavaScript function that receives the event name string
- Example:
```js
hs.audiodevice.addWatcher((event) => {
    if (event === "dOut") console.log("Default output changed")
})
```
methods.description: Register a listener for all system-level audio configuration events.

params.name: listener
params.description: A JavaScript function that receives the event name string

methods.name: removeWatcher
methods.rawDocumentation:
Remove a previously registered system-level listener.

- Parameter listener: The JavaScript function that was passed to ``addWatcher(_:)``
- Example:
```js
hs.audiodevice.removeWatcher(myHandler)
```
methods.description: Remove a previously registered system-level listener.

params.name: listener
params.description: The JavaScript function that was passed to ``addWatcher(_:)``

methods.name: addWatcher
methods.rawDocumentation:
Register a listener for a per-device property-change event.

The callback receives one of these event strings:
- `"vmout"` — output volume changed
- `"vmin"` — input volume changed
- `"mout"` — output mute state changed
- `"min"` — input mute state changed
- `"rate"` — sample rate changed
- `"dsout"` — output data source changed
- `"dsin"` — input data source changed

- Parameter listener: {(event: string) => void} A JavaScript function that receives an event name string
- Example:
```js
const dev = hs.audiodevice.defaultOutputDevice()
dev.addWatcher((event) => console.log("Event:", event))
```
methods.description: Register a listener for a per-device property-change event.

params.name: listener
params.description: A JavaScript function that receives an event name string

examples.code:
const dev = hs.audiodevice.defaultOutputDevice()
dev.addWatcher((event) => console.log("Event:", event))

methods.name: removeWatcher
methods.rawDocumentation:
Remove a previously registered per-device listener.

- Parameter listener: The JavaScript function that was passed to ``addWatcher(_:)``
- Example:
```js
const dev = hs.audiodevice.defaultOutputDevice()
dev.removeWatcher(myHandler)
```
methods.description: Remove a previously registered per-device listener.

params.name: listener
params.description: The JavaScript function that was passed to ``addWatcher(_:)``

properties.rawDocumentation:
Output volume scalar in the range `0.0`–`1.0`, or `null` if the device has

properties.description:
Output volume scalar in the range `0.0`–`1.0`, or `null` if the device has
no controllable output volume. Setting `null` is a no-op.

properties.name: muted
properties.rawDocumentation:
Whether output is muted. Always `false` if the device has no mutable output.
- Example:

properties.description: Whether output is muted. Always `false` if the device has no mutable output.

properties.rawDocumentation:
Output stereo balance in the range `0.0` (full left)–`1.0` (full right),

properties.name: inputVolume
properties.rawDocumentation:
Input (microphone) volume scalar in the range `0.0`–`1.0`, or `null` if
the device has no controllable input volume.
- Example:

properties.description:
Input (microphone) volume scalar in the range `0.0`–`1.0`, or `null` if
the device has no controllable input volume.

properties.name: inputMuted
properties.rawDocumentation:
Whether input is muted. Always `false` if the device has no mutable input.
- Example:
```js

mic.inputMuted = true
```
properties.description: Whether input is muted. Always `false` if the device has no mutable input.

properties.name: sampleRate
properties.rawDocumentation:
The current nominal sample rate in Hz (e.g. `44100`), or `null` if unknown.
- Example:

All sample rates (in Hz) that this device supports.

methods.rawDocumentation:
Add a watcher for application AX events

returns.description: An HSAXElement representing the focused UI element, or null if none was found

methods.name: findByRole
methods.rawDocumentation:
Find AX elements for a given role
Parameters:
- role: The role name to search for
- parent: An HSAXElement object to search. If none is supplied, the search will be conducted system-wide
- options: Optional object: { maxDepth: number, maxNodes: number }. maxDepth limits how many levels below the search root are visited (0 = the root only; unlimited when omitted). maxNodes caps the total number of elements visited (default 10000; pass 0 for unlimited). Every AX call is a synchronous IPC round-trip on the JS thread, so an uncapped walk of a large app (or the system-wide element) can freeze Hammerspoon for minutes — the default cap turns that into a bounded, warned-about truncation.
Returns: An array of found elements
Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.frontmost()
const buttons = hs.ax.findByRole(app, "AXButton")
const windows = hs.ax.findByRole('AXWindow', appElem, { maxDepth: 1 })
```
methods.description: Find AX elements for a given role

params.name: role
params.description: The role name to search for

params.name: parent
params.description: An HSAXElement object to search. If none is supplied, the search will be conducted system-wide

params.name: options
params.description: Optional object: { maxDepth: number, maxNodes: number }. maxDepth limits how many levels below the search root are visited (0 = the root only; unlimited when omitted). maxNodes caps the total number of elements visited (default 10000; pass 0 for unlimited). Every AX call is a synchronous IPC round-trip on the JS thread, so an uncapped walk of a large app (or the system-wide element) can freeze Hammerspoon for minutes — the default cap turns that into a bounded, warned-about truncation.

methods.name: findByTitle
methods.rawDocumentation:
Find AX elements by title
Parameters:
- title: The name to search for
- parent: An HSAXElement object to search. If none is supplied, the search will be conducted system-wide
- options: Optional object: { maxDepth: number, maxNodes: number }. maxDepth limits how many levels below the search root are visited (0 = the root only; unlimited when omitted). maxNodes caps the total number of elements visited (default 10000; pass 0 for unlimited). Every AX call is a synchronous IPC round-trip on the JS thread, so an uncapped walk of a large app (or the system-wide element) can freeze Hammerspoon for minutes — the default cap turns that into a bounded, warned-about truncation.
Returns: An array of found elements
Example:
```js
const app = hs.application.frontmost()
const matches = hs.ax.findByTitle(app, "OK")
const shallow = hs.ax.findByTitle('OK', appElem, { maxDepth: 4 })
```
methods.description: Find AX elements by title

params.name: title
params.description: The name to search for

params.name: parent
params.description: An HSAXElement object to search. If none is supplied, the search will be conducted system-wide

params.name: options
params.description: Optional object: { maxDepth: number, maxNodes: number }. maxDepth limits how many levels below the search root are visited (0 = the root only; unlimited when omitted). maxNodes caps the total number of elements visited (default 10000; pass 0 for unlimited). Every AX call is a synchronous IPC round-trip on the JS thread, so an uncapped walk of a large app (or the system-wide element) can freeze Hammerspoon for minutes — the default cap turns that into a bounded, warned-about truncation.

modules.name: hs.ble

methods.name: central
methods.rawDocumentation:
Create the BLE central used to attach to the bonded ESP32 relay service.

Each call returns a fresh `HSBLECentral`; in practice one central is enough.
All centrals are torn down when the module shuts down (JS reload).
- Returns: an `HSBLECentral`.
- Example:
```js
const c = hs.ble.central()
c.onState(s => console.log('ble', s))
```
methods.description:
Create the BLE central used to attach to the bonded ESP32 relay service.
Each call returns a fresh `HSBLECentral`; in practice one central is enough.
All centrals are torn down when the module shuts down (JS reload).

examples.code:
const c = hs.ble.central()
c.onState(s => console.log('ble', s))

types.name: HSBLECentralAPI
types.rawDocumentation:
A CoreBluetooth central that attaches to the already-bonded ESP32 relay service.

Obtain via `hs.ble.central()`. Register `onState`, then `connect(...)` to attach
to the bonded peripheral and start the relay. The returned `HSBLEPeripheral`
reports connection and notify events.
types.description:
A CoreBluetooth central that attaches to the already-bonded ESP32 relay service.
Obtain via `hs.ble.central()`. Register `onState`, then `connect(...)` to attach
to the bonded peripheral and start the relay. The returned `HSBLEPeripheral`
reports connection and notify events.

methods.name: onState
methods.rawDocumentation:
Register a callback for CoreBluetooth manager state changes.

Fires immediately with the current state if one is already known, then again
on every change. Use it to wait for `"poweredOn"` before calling `connect`,
and to surface `"unauthorized"` (missing Bluetooth permission) to the user.
- Parameter cb: `function(state)` — one of `"poweredOn"`, `"poweredOff"`,
  `"unauthorized"`, `"unsupported"`, `"resetting"`, `"unknown"`.
- Returns: self, for chaining.
- Example:
```js
hs.ble.central().onState(s => { if (s === 'poweredOn') connect() })
```
methods.description:
Register a callback for CoreBluetooth manager state changes.
Fires immediately with the current state if one is already known, then again
on every change. Use it to wait for `"poweredOn"` before calling `connect`,
and to surface `"unauthorized"` (missing Bluetooth permission) to the user.
`"unauthorized"`, `"unsupported"`, `"resetting"`, `"unknown"`.

params.name: cb
params.description: `function(state)` — one of `"poweredOn"`, `"poweredOff"`,

methods.name: connect
methods.rawDocumentation:
Attach to the bonded ESP32 relay peripheral and bring up the relay channel.

Connection strategy (mirrors the proven helper): fast-attach via
`retrievePeripherals` when a `peerUUID` is known, else
`retrieveConnectedPeripherals`, else scan. Discovers the relay service,
subscribes the notify characteristic, and fires the peripheral's
`onConnect` once subscribed. Auto-reconnects on drop unless `autoReconnect`
is `false`.
- Parameter config: `{ name?, peerUUID?, service?, notifyChar?, writeChar?, autoReconnect? }`.
  `name` defaults to `"VoiceKB"`; the UUIDs default to the firmware relay
  UUIDs; `autoReconnect` defaults to `true`. Pass `peerUUID` (from a prior
  `peripheral.uuid`) for instant re-attach to the non-advertising bonded peer.
- Returns: an `HSBLEPeripheral`.
- Example:
```js
const p = c.connect({ name: 'VoiceKB', peerUUID: saved, autoReconnect: true })
p.onNotify(line => relay.ingest(line))
```
methods.description:
Attach to the bonded ESP32 relay peripheral and bring up the relay channel.
Connection strategy (mirrors the proven helper): fast-attach via
`retrievePeripherals` when a `peerUUID` is known, else
`retrieveConnectedPeripherals`, else scan. Discovers the relay service,
subscribes the notify characteristic, and fires the peripheral's
`onConnect` once subscribed. Auto-reconnects on drop unless `autoReconnect`
is `false`.
`name` defaults to `"VoiceKB"`; the UUIDs default to the firmware relay
UUIDs; `autoReconnect` defaults to `true`. Pass `peerUUID` (from a prior
`peripheral.uuid`) for instant re-attach to the non-advertising bonded peer.

params.name: config
params.description: `{ name?, peerUUID?, service?, notifyChar?, writeChar?, autoReconnect? }`.

examples.code:
const p = c.connect({ name: 'VoiceKB', peerUUID: saved, autoReconnect: true })
p.onNotify(line => relay.ingest(line))

types.name: HSBLEPeripheralAPI
types.rawDocumentation:
A handle to the relay peripheral on the bonded ESP32.

Returned by `HSBLECentral.connect(...)`. Register `onConnect` / `onDisconnect` /
`onNotify`, and `write` lines to the controller. `onConnect` fires once the relay
notify characteristic is subscribed (the channel is live).
types.description:
A handle to the relay peripheral on the bonded ESP32.
Returned by `HSBLECentral.connect(...)`. Register `onConnect` / `onDisconnect` /
`onNotify`, and `write` lines to the controller. `onConnect` fires once the relay
notify characteristic is subscribed (the channel is live).

methods.name: onConnect
methods.rawDocumentation:
Register a callback fired when the relay channel becomes live (notify subscribed).
- Parameter cb: `function()`.
- Returns: self, for chaining.
- Example:
```js
p.onConnect(() => console.log('relay up'))
```
methods.description: Register a callback fired when the relay channel becomes live (notify subscribed).

params.name: cb
params.description: `function()`.

methods.name: onDisconnect
methods.rawDocumentation:
Register a callback fired when the peripheral disconnects.
- Parameter cb: `function(reason)` — `reason` is the disconnect error text, or `"clean"`.
- Returns: self, for chaining.
- Example:
```js
p.onDisconnect(reason => console.log('relay down:', reason))
```
methods.description: Register a callback fired when the peripheral disconnects.

params.name: cb
params.description: `function(reason)` — `reason` is the disconnect error text, or `"clean"`.

methods.name: onNotify
methods.rawDocumentation:
Register a callback fired for each notify payload from the controller.
- Parameter cb: `function(line)` — `line` is the UTF-8 payload (a JSON string the relay layer parses).
- Returns: self, for chaining.
- Example:
```js
p.onNotify(line => { const msg = JSON.parse(line); /* ... */ })
```
methods.description: Register a callback fired for each notify payload from the controller.

params.name: cb
params.description: `function(line)` — `line` is the UTF-8 payload (a JSON string the relay layer parses).

methods.name: write
methods.rawDocumentation:
Write a line to the relay write characteristic (target → controller), `.withoutResponse`.
- Parameter s: the UTF-8 payload (caller-supplied JSON; keep it under the ATT MTU, ~240 B).
- Returns: `true` if queued; `false` if not connected or the payload exceeds the MTU.
- Example:
```js
p.write(JSON.stringify({ clip: 'hello' }))
```
methods.description: Write a line to the relay write characteristic (target → controller), `.withoutResponse`.

params.name: s
params.description: the UTF-8 payload (caller-supplied JSON; keep it under the ATT MTU, ~240 B).

returns.description: `true` if queued; `false` if not connected or the payload exceeds the MTU.

methods.name: disconnect
methods.rawDocumentation:
Disconnect the peripheral and stop auto-reconnect.
- Example:
```js
p.disconnect()
```
methods.description: Disconnect the peripheral and stop auto-reconnect.

properties.name: uuid
properties.rawDocumentation:
The peripheral's system UUID. Persist this (e.g. in your config) and pass it
back as `connect({ peerUUID })` for instant re-attach to the non-advertising
bonded peer. Empty until first connected.
- Example:
```js
p.onConnect(() => saveConfig({ peerUUID: p.uuid }))
```
properties.description:
The peripheral's system UUID. Persist this (e.g. in your config) and pass it
back as `connect({ peerUUID })` for instant re-attach to the non-advertising
bonded peer. Empty until first connected.

modules.description:
Module providing a single CoreBluetooth central for the CrossMac control relay.
On the **target** Mac, this module attaches to the ESP32 "VoiceKB" peripheral that
the OS is already BLE-bonded to for HID, discovers the custom relay GATT service,
subscribes its notify characteristic (controller → target) and writes its write
characteristic (target → controller). It never touches HID — the OS owns that.
This is deliberately *not* a general GATT stack: one service, one notify char, one
write char, on a device we are already bonded to.
modules.rawDocumentation:
Module providing a single CoreBluetooth central for the CrossMac control relay.

On the **target** Mac, this module attaches to the ESP32 "VoiceKB" peripheral that
the OS is already BLE-bonded to for HID, discovers the custom relay GATT service,
subscribes its notify characteristic (controller → target) and writes its write
characteristic (target → controller). It never touches HID — the OS owns that.

This is deliberately *not* a general GATT stack: one service, one notify char, one
write char, on a device we are already bonded to.

modules.name: hs.bonjour

methods.name: createSearch
methods.description:
Creates a new Bonjour search for discovering services or domains.
Call one of the `find…` methods on the returned search to start
discovering. Remove it with `removeSearch()` when finished.

methods.name: removeSearch
methods.rawDocumentation:
Stops and removes a previously created search.
- Parameter search: the search returned by `createSearch()`
- Example:
```js
const s = hs.bonjour.createSearch()
// ... use search ...
hs.bonjour.removeSearch(s)
```
methods.description: Stops and removes a previously created search.

params.name: search
params.description: the search returned by `createSearch()`

methods.name: advertise
methods.signature: func advertise(_ name: String, _ type: String, _ port: Int, _ domain: String, _ callback: JSFunction)
methods.rawDocumentation:
Starts advertising a local service on the network.

The optional callback receives `(event)` or `(event, errorMessage)`:
- `"published"` — now advertising
- `"stopped"` — advertisement stopped
- `"error"` — publication failed; error message in second argument

If `domain` is omitted or not a string, it defaults to `"local."`.
If the 4th argument is a function, it is used as the callback and domain
defaults to `"local."`.
- Parameter name: human-readable name shown to browsers (e.g. `"My Web Server"`)
- Parameter type: service type in `"_proto._tcp."` or `"_proto._udp."` form
- Parameter port: port number the service listens on
- Parameter domain: mDNS domain; defaults to `"local."` if an empty string is passed
- Parameter callback?: {((event: string, error?: string) => void) | null} Optional function called on status changes with event name and optional error message
- Example:
```js
hs.bonjour.advertise('My Server', '_http._tcp.', 8080, '', ev => {
    if (ev === 'published') console.log('Now advertising!')
})
```
methods.description:
Starts advertising a local service on the network.
If `domain` is omitted or not a string, it defaults to `"local."`.
If the 4th argument is a function, it is used as the callback and domain
defaults to `"local."`.

params.name: name
params.description: human-readable name shown to browsers (e.g. `"My Web Server"`)

params.name: type
params.description: service type in `"_proto._tcp."` or `"_proto._udp."` form

params.name: port
params.description: port number the service listens on

params.name: domain
params.description: mDNS domain; defaults to `"local."` if an empty string is passed

params.name: callback
params.description: Optional function called on status changes with event name and optional error message

methods.name: stopAdvertising
methods.signature: func stopAdvertising(_ name: String, _ type: String)

Stops advertising a service previously started with `advertise()`.
- Parameter name: the name passed to `advertise()`
- Parameter type: the type passed to `advertise()`
- Example:
```js
hs.bonjour.stopAdvertising('My Server', '_http._tcp.')
```
methods.description: Stops advertising a service previously started with `advertise()`.

params.name: name
params.description: the name passed to `advertise()`

params.name: type
params.description: the type passed to `advertise()`

methods.name: networkServices
methods.rawDocumentation:
Returns a Promise that resolves to an array of service-type strings
currently advertised on the local network.

Internally searches for `_services._dns-sd._udp.` services, collects
results for up to `timeout` seconds (or until the browser signals no more
results), then resolves.
- Parameter timeout: maximum seconds to wait (pass `0` to use the default 5 s)
- Returns: {Promise<string[]>} a Promise resolving to an array of service-type strings such as `"_http._tcp."`
- Example:
```js
hs.bonjour.networkServices(5).then(types => {
    console.log('Active service types: ' + types.join(', '))
})
```
methods.description:
Returns a Promise that resolves to an array of service-type strings
currently advertised on the local network.
Internally searches for `_services._dns-sd._udp.` services, collects
results for up to `timeout` seconds (or until the browser signals no more
results), then resolves.

params.name: timeout
params.description: maximum seconds to wait (pass `0` to use the default 5 s)

returns.description: a Promise resolving to an array of service-type strings such as `"_http._tcp."`

properties.name: serviceTypes
properties.rawDocumentation:
A frozen object mapping short service-type names to their mDNS strings.

Populated by the JavaScript enhancement layer.
- Example:
```js
console.log(hs.bonjour.serviceTypes.ssh)  // "_ssh._tcp."
```
properties.description:
A frozen object mapping short service-type names to their mDNS strings.
Populated by the JavaScript enhancement layer.

types.name: HSBonjourSearchAPI
types.rawDocumentation:
Discovers Bonjour services and domains advertised on the local network.

Create via `hs.bonjour.newSearch()`, then call one of the `find…` methods.
Each search type uses its own underlying `NetServiceBrowser`, so service and
domain searches can run concurrently. Restarting any single search type stops
only that browser before beginning the new one.

## Service search callback events

| Event | Data | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `"serviceFound"` | `HSBonjourService` | A matching service appeared |
| `"serviceRemoved"` | `HSBonjourService` | A previously found service disappeared |
| `"error"` | error string | The search failed |

## Domain search callback events

| Event | Data | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `"domainFound"` | domain string | A domain was discovered |
| `"domainRemoved"` | domain string | A domain disappeared |
| `"error"` | error string | The search failed |

Example:
```js
const search = hs.bonjour.newSearch()
search.findServices('_ssh._tcp.', 'local.', (event, svc, moreComing) => {
    if (event !== 'serviceFound') return
    console.log('Discovered: ' + svc.name + ' (' + svc.type + ' ' + svc.domain + ')')
    svc.resolve(5, ev => {
        if (ev === 'error') { console.error('Resolve failed for' + svc.name); return }
        if (ev !== 'resolved') return
        console.log('  Host:      ' + svc.hostname + ' port ' + svc.port)
        console.log('  Addresses: ' + svc.addresses.join(', '))
        if (svc.txtRecord) console.log('  TXT record:', JSON.stringify(svc.txtRecord))
    })
})
```
types.description:
Discovers Bonjour services and domains advertised on the local network.
Create via `hs.bonjour.newSearch()`, then call one of the `find…` methods.
Each search type uses its own underlying `NetServiceBrowser`, so service and
domain searches can run concurrently. Restarting any single search type stops
only that browser before beginning the new one.
## Service search callback events
| Event | Data | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `"serviceFound"` | `HSBonjourService` | A matching service appeared |
| `"serviceRemoved"` | `HSBonjourService` | A previously found service disappeared |
| `"error"` | error string | The search failed |
## Domain search callback events
| Event | Data | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `"domainFound"` | domain string | A domain was discovered |
| `"domainRemoved"` | domain string | A domain disappeared |
| `"error"` | error string | The search failed |

methods.name: findServices
methods.signature: func findServices(_ type: String, _ domain: String, _ callback: JSFunction) -> HSBonjourSearch
methods.rawDocumentation:
Searches for services of the given type in the given domain.

If a service search is already active it is stopped before starting the
new one. Domain searches are unaffected. The callback receives
`(event, service, moreComing)` — see the type documentation for the
complete event table.
- Parameter type: service type string, e.g. `"_http._tcp."` or `"_ssh._tcp."`
- Parameter domain: mDNS domain; `"local."` for the local link, `""` for all domains
- Parameter callback: {(event: string, service: HSBonjourService, moreComing: boolean) => void} Called for each result with event name, service object, and whether more results are expected
- Returns: self, for chaining
- Example:
```js
search.findServices('_http._tcp.', 'local.', (ev, svc, more) => {
    if (ev === 'serviceFound') console.log('Found:', svc.name)
})
```
methods.description:
Searches for services of the given type in the given domain.
If a service search is already active it is stopped before starting the
new one. Domain searches are unaffected. The callback receives
`(event, service, moreComing)` — see the type documentation for the
complete event table.

params.name: type
params.description: service type string, e.g. `"_http._tcp."` or `"_ssh._tcp."`

params.name: domain
params.description: mDNS domain; `"local."` for the local link, `""` for all domains

params.name: callback
params.description: Called for each result with event name, service object, and whether more results are expected
params.tsType: (event: string, service: HSBonjourService, moreComing: boolean) => void

methods.name: findBrowsableDomains
methods.rawDocumentation:
Searches for domains visible to this machine (browsable domains).

If a browsable-domain search is already active it is stopped before
starting the new one. Service and registration-domain searches are
unaffected. The callback receives `(event, domain, moreComing)`.
- Parameter callback: {(event: string, domain: string, moreComing: boolean) => void} Called for each result with event name, domain string, and whether more results are expected
- Returns: self, for chaining
- Example:
```js
search.findBrowsableDomains((ev, domain, more) => {
    if (ev === 'domainFound') console.log('Domain:', domain)
})
```
methods.description:
Searches for domains visible to this machine (browsable domains).
If a browsable-domain search is already active it is stopped before
starting the new one. Service and registration-domain searches are
unaffected. The callback receives `(event, domain, moreComing)`.

params.name: callback
params.description: Called for each result with event name, domain string, and whether more results are expected
params.tsType: (event: string, domain: string, moreComing: boolean) => void

methods.name: findRegistrationDomains
methods.rawDocumentation:
Searches for domains on which this machine can register services.

If a registration-domain search is already active it is stopped before
starting the new one. Service and browsable-domain searches are
unaffected. The callback receives `(event, domain, moreComing)`.
- Parameter callback: {(event: string, domain: string, moreComing: boolean) => void} Called for each result with event name, domain string, and whether more results are expected
- Returns: self, for chaining
- Example:
```js
search.findRegistrationDomains((ev, domain, more) => {
    if (ev === 'domainFound') console.log('Can register in:', domain)
})
```
methods.description:
Searches for domains on which this machine can register services.
If a registration-domain search is already active it is stopped before
starting the new one. Service and browsable-domain searches are
unaffected. The callback receives `(event, domain, moreComing)`.

params.name: callback
params.description: Called for each result with event name, domain string, and whether more results are expected
params.tsType: (event: string, domain: string, moreComing: boolean) => void

methods.name: stop
methods.rawDocumentation:
Stops all active searches. Safe to call when no search is active.
- Returns: self, for chaining
- Example:
```js
search.stop()
```
methods.description: Stops all active searches. Safe to call when no search is active.

properties.rawDocumentation:
A unique identifier for this search object.
- Example:
```js
console.log(hs.bonjour.newSearch().identifier)
```
properties.description: A unique identifier for this search object.

properties.rawDocumentation:
Whether to search over peer-to-peer Bluetooth/Wi-Fi in addition to
standard network interfaces. Defaults to `false`.
- Example:
```js
search.includesPeerToPeer = true
```

Whether to search over peer-to-peer Bluetooth/Wi-Fi in addition to

types.name: HSBonjourServiceAPI
types.rawDocumentation:
A discovered Bonjour service record. Call `resolve()` to look up its
hostname, port, and addresses.

Instances are delivered by an `HSBonjourSearch` callback. Call `resolve()`
to discover their hostname, port, and addresses, and optionally `monitor()`
to watch for TXT record changes.

## Callback events

| Method | Event | Extra data |
|--------|-------|------------|
| `resolve()` | `"resolved"` | _(none)_ |
| `resolve()` | `"stopped"` | _(none)_ |
| `resolve()` | `"error"` | error message string |
| `monitor()` | `"txtRecord"` | updated TXT record dict |
types.description:
A discovered Bonjour service record. Call `resolve()` to look up its
hostname, port, and addresses.
Instances are delivered by an `HSBonjourSearch` callback. Call `resolve()`
to discover their hostname, port, and addresses, and optionally `monitor()`
to watch for TXT record changes.
## Callback events
| Method | Event | Extra data |
|--------|-------|------------|
| `resolve()` | `"resolved"` | _(none)_ |
| `resolve()` | `"stopped"` | _(none)_ |
| `resolve()` | `"error"` | error message string |
| `monitor()` | `"txtRecord"` | updated TXT record dict |

methods.name: resolve
methods.signature: func resolve(_ timeout: Double, _ callback: JSFunction) -> HSBonjourService
methods.rawDocumentation:
Resolves the hostname, port, addresses, and TXT record of this service.

The callback is called with `(event)` or `(event, errorMessage)`:
- `"resolved"` — resolution complete; read `hostname`, `port`, `addresses`, `txtRecord`
- `"stopped"` — resolution stopped before completing
- `"error"` — resolution failed; error message in second argument
- Parameter timeout: seconds before giving up; pass `0` for no timeout
- Parameter callback: {(event: string, error?: string) => void} Called on status changes with event name and optional error message
- Returns: self, for chaining
- Example:
```js
service.resolve(5, (ev, err) => {
    if (ev === 'resolved') console.log(service.hostname, service.port)
    else console.error('Resolve failed:', err)
})
```
methods.description: Resolves the hostname, port, addresses, and TXT record of this service.

params.name: timeout
params.description: seconds before giving up; pass `0` for no timeout

params.name: callback
params.description: Called on status changes with event name and optional error message

methods.name: monitor
methods.rawDocumentation:
Starts monitoring the TXT record for changes. The callback fires whenever
the TXT record is updated.

Call `stopMonitoring()` to unsubscribe.
- Parameter callback: {(txtRecord: Record<string, string>) => void} Called when TXT data changes with the updated record
- Returns: self, for chaining
- Example:
```js
service.monitor(txt => console.log('TXT updated:', txt))
```
methods.description:
Starts monitoring the TXT record for changes. The callback fires whenever
the TXT record is updated.
Call `stopMonitoring()` to unsubscribe.

params.name: callback
params.description: Called when TXT data changes with the updated record

methods.name: stop
methods.rawDocumentation:
Stops any active resolution.
- Returns: self, for chaining
- Example:
```js
service.stop()
```
methods.description: Stops any active resolution.

methods.name: stopMonitoring
methods.rawDocumentation:
Stops TXT record monitoring started by `monitor()`.
- Returns: self, for chaining
- Example:
```js
service.stopMonitoring()
```

modules.name: hs.calendar

methods.name: authorizationStatus
methods.rawDocumentation:
Return the app's current Calendar authorization status.
- Returns: One of `fullAccess`, `writeOnly`, `denied`, `restricted`, or `notDetermined`
- Example:
```js
const status = hs.calendar.authorizationStatus()
console.log(status)
```
methods.description: Return the app's current Calendar authorization status.

methods.name: listCalendars
methods.rawDocumentation:
List the Calendars available for Events.
- Returns: Calendar summaries containing `id`, `title`, `writable`, and `isDefault`
- Example:
```js
for (const calendar of hs.calendar.listCalendars()) {
  console.log(calendar.title, calendar.id)
}
```
methods.description: List the Calendars available for Events.

methods.name: listEvents
methods.signature: func listEvents(_ calendar: String, _ start: String, _ end: String) -> [[String: Any]]
methods.rawDocumentation:
List Events from one Calendar that overlap a time window. The window may span at most four years.
- Parameters:
  - calendar: Calendar id or exact title
  - start: Window start as a valid ISO 8601 datetime with an explicit offset from `-14:00` through `+14:00`, or `Z`
  - end: Window end as a valid ISO 8601 datetime with an explicit offset from `-14:00` through `+14:00`, or `Z`
- Returns: Event objects containing `id`, `title`, `start`, `end`, `allDay`, `location`, `notes`, `url`, `attendees`, `organizer`, `status`, `availability`, `recurring`, and `occurrenceStart`. Timed values use UTC `Z`; all-day values are `YYYY-MM-DD`. Each attendee and organizer contains `name`, `url`, `status`, `role`, `type`, and `currentUser`. Non-recurring Events have a `null` `occurrenceStart`.
- Example:
```js
const events = hs.calendar.listEvents(
  'Work',
  '2026-07-12T00:00:00Z',
  '2026-07-13T00:00:00Z'
)
```
methods.description: List Events from one Calendar that overlap a time window. The window may span at most four years.

params.name: calendar
params.description: Calendar id or exact title

params.name: start
params.description: Window start as a valid ISO 8601 datetime with an explicit offset from `-14:00` through `+14:00`, or `Z`

params.name: end
params.description: Window end as a valid ISO 8601 datetime with an explicit offset from `-14:00` through `+14:00`, or `Z`

returns.description: Event objects containing `id`, `title`, `start`, `end`, `allDay`, `location`, `notes`, `url`, `attendees`, `organizer`, `status`, `availability`, `recurring`, and `occurrenceStart`. Timed values use UTC `Z`; all-day values are `YYYY-MM-DD`. Each attendee and organizer contains `name`, `url`, `status`, `role`, `type`, and `currentUser`. Non-recurring Events have a `null` `occurrenceStart`.

methods.name: searchEvents
methods.signature: func searchEvents(_ query: String, _ start: String, _ end: String) -> [[String: Any]]
methods.rawDocumentation:
Search Event titles across all Calendars in a time window. The window may span at most four years.
- Parameters:
  - query: Case-insensitive text to find in Event titles
  - start: Window start as a valid ISO 8601 datetime with an explicit offset from `-14:00` through `+14:00`, or `Z`
  - end: Window end as a valid ISO 8601 datetime with an explicit offset from `-14:00` through `+14:00`, or `Z`
- Returns: Matching Event objects in the same occurrence-aware shape as `listEvents`
- Example:
```js
const events = hs.calendar.searchEvents(
  'planning',
  '2026-07-12T00:00:00Z',
  '2026-07-19T00:00:00Z'
)
```
methods.description: Search Event titles across all Calendars in a time window. The window may span at most four years.

params.name: query
params.description: Case-insensitive text to find in Event titles

params.name: start
params.description: Window start as a valid ISO 8601 datetime with an explicit offset from `-14:00` through `+14:00`, or `Z`

params.name: end
params.description: Window end as a valid ISO 8601 datetime with an explicit offset from `-14:00` through `+14:00`, or `Z`

returns.description: Matching Event objects in the same occurrence-aware shape as `listEvents`

methods.name: createEvent
methods.rawDocumentation:
Create a single Event.
- Parameter options: Event fields: `title`, `start`, `end`, optional `calendar`, `allDay`, `location`, `notes`, `url`, and `alarms`
  Timed `start`/`end` values require an explicit UTC offset or `Z`; all-day values must be `YYYY-MM-DD`.
  `calendar` resolves by id first, then exact title, and defaults to the Calendar for new Events when omitted.
  `alarms` contains non-negative minutes-before values. Recurring Event creation is unsupported.
- Returns: The created Event as a plain object; invalid input, unavailable targets, and save failures throw a JavaScript `Error`
- Example:
```js
const event = hs.calendar.createEvent({
  title: 'Project check-in',
  start: '2026-07-13T01:00:00Z',
  end: '2026-07-13T01:30:00Z',
  alarms: [10]
})
console.log(event.id)
```
methods.description:
Create a single Event.
Timed `start`/`end` values require an explicit UTC offset or `Z`; all-day values must be `YYYY-MM-DD`.
`calendar` resolves by id first, then exact title, and defaults to the Calendar for new Events when omitted.
`alarms` contains non-negative minutes-before values. Recurring Event creation is unsupported.

params.name: options
params.description: Event fields: `title`, `start`, `end`, optional `calendar`, `allDay`, `location`, `notes`, `url`, and `alarms`

returns.description: The created Event as a plain object; invalid input, unavailable targets, and save failures throw a JavaScript `Error`

types.name: HSCameraAPI
types.rawDocumentation:
A camera device attached to the system.

Obtain instances via the ``hs.camera`` module — do not instantiate directly.

## Reading camera properties

```javascript
const cam = hs.camera.all()[0]
console.log(cam.name + " uid=" + cam.uid + " inUse=" + cam.isInUse)
```

## Watching for in-use state changes

```javascript
const cam = hs.camera.all()[0]
const fn = (isInUse) => {
    console.log(cam.name + " is now " + (isInUse ? "in use" : "not in use"))
}
cam.addWatcher(fn)
// later…
cam.removeWatcher(fn)
```

## Capturing a still image

```javascript
const cam = hs.camera.all()[0]
cam.captureImage()
    .then(img => img.saveToFile("/tmp/shot.png"))
    .catch(err => console.error("Capture failed: " + err))
```
types.description:

Obtain instances via the ``hs.camera`` module — do not instantiate directly.

```javascript
const cam = hs.camera.all()[0]
console.log(cam.name + " uid=" + cam.uid + " inUse=" + cam.isInUse)
```

```javascript

const fn = (isInUse) => {
    console.log(cam.name + " is now " + (isInUse ? "in use" : "not in use"))
}
cam.addWatcher(fn)
// later…
cam.removeWatcher(fn)
```
## Capturing a still image
```javascript
const cam = hs.camera.all()[0]

    .then(img => img.saveToFile("/tmp/shot.png"))
    .catch(err => console.error("Capture failed: " + err))
```

methods.name: addWatcher
methods.rawDocumentation:
Register a listener that fires whenever this camera's in-use state changes.

The listener receives one argument: a boolean that is `true` when the camera
starts being used and `false` when it is released.

- Parameter listener: {(isInUse: boolean) => void} A JavaScript function called with `true` when the camera starts being used and `false` when released
- Example:
```js
const cam = hs.camera.all()[0]
cam.addWatcher((inUse) => {
    console.log(cam.name + " is " + (inUse ? "now in use" : "no longer in use"))
})
```
methods.description:
Register a listener that fires whenever this camera's in-use state changes.
The listener receives one argument: a boolean that is `true` when the camera
starts being used and `false` when it is released.

params.name: listener
params.description: A JavaScript function called with `true` when the camera starts being used and `false` when released

methods.name: removeWatcher
methods.rawDocumentation:
Remove a previously registered per-camera in-use listener.
- Parameter listener: The function originally passed to ``addWatcher(_:)``
- Example:
```js
cam.removeWatcher(myHandler)
```
methods.description: Remove a previously registered per-camera in-use listener.

params.name: listener
params.description: The function originally passed to ``addWatcher(_:)``

methods.name: captureImage
methods.rawDocumentation:
Capture a still image from this camera.

Camera permission must be granted via `hs.permissions.requestCamera()` before calling
this method. The returned `HSImage` can be saved, displayed in a UI element, or
passed to other image-processing APIs.

- Returns: {Promise<HSImage>} A Promise that resolves to an `HSImage`, or rejects on error
- Example:
```js
const img = await hs.camera.all()[0].captureImage()
img.saveToFile("/tmp/snapshot.png")
```
methods.description:
Capture a still image from this camera.
Camera permission must be granted via `hs.permissions.requestCamera()` before calling
this method. The returned `HSImage` can be saved, displayed in a UI element, or
passed to other image-processing APIs.

returns.description: A Promise that resolves to an `HSImage`, or rejects on error

properties.name: typeName
properties.rawDocumentation: The type name for JavaScript introspection. Always `"HSCamera"`.
properties.description: The type name for JavaScript introspection. Always `"HSCamera"`.

properties.name: uid
properties.rawDocumentation:
The persistent unique identifier for this camera.
- Example:

properties.description: The persistent unique identifier for this camera.

properties.name: name
properties.rawDocumentation:

modules.name: hs.chooser

methods.name: create
methods.rawDocumentation:
Create a new chooser.
- Returns: A new `HSChooser` object ready for configuration
- Example:
```js
const c = hs.chooser.create()
c.setChoices([{text: "Hello"}]).onSelect = item => console.log(item.text)
c.show()
```
methods.description: Create a new chooser.

returns.description: A new `HSChooser` object ready for configuration

types.name: HSChooserAPI
types.rawDocumentation:
A keyboard-driven floating chooser panel.

Create via `hs.chooser.create()`. Configure choices, set callbacks, then call `.show()`.

## Choice format

Each choice is a plain object with required `text` and optional `subText`, `image`, `valid`,
and `contextMenu` fields. All other fields are passed through to the `onSelect` callback unchanged.

The `contextMenu` array defines per-row right-click menu entries. Each entry is either
`{ title: "...", action: (item) => {} }` for a button or `{ type: "divider" }` for a separator:

```javascript
{
  text: "Open Safari", subText: "com.apple.Safari",
  image: HSImage.fromAppBundle("com.apple.Safari"), valid: true, myData: 42,
  contextMenu: [
    { title: "Open", action: () => hs.urlevent.openURL("https://apple.com") },
    { type: "divider" },
    { title: "Copy bundle ID", action: () => hs.pasteboard.writeString("com.apple.Safari") }
  ]
}
```

## Keyboard shortcuts

- **Return** — confirm selection
- **Escape** — dismiss (calls `onSelect` with `null`)
- **↑ / ↓** — move through results
types.description:
A keyboard-driven floating chooser panel.
Create via `hs.chooser.create()`. Configure choices, set callbacks, then call `.show()`.
## Choice format
Each choice is a plain object with required `text` and optional `subText`, `image`, `valid`,
and `contextMenu` fields. All other fields are passed through to the `onSelect` callback unchanged.
The `contextMenu` array defines per-row right-click menu entries. Each entry is either
```javascript
{
  text: "Open Safari", subText: "com.apple.Safari",
  image: HSImage.fromAppBundle("com.apple.Safari"), valid: true, myData: 42,
  contextMenu: [
    { title: "Open", action: () => hs.urlevent.openURL("https://apple.com") },
    { type: "divider" },
    { title: "Copy bundle ID", action: () => hs.pasteboard.writeString("com.apple.Safari") }
  ]
}
```
## Keyboard shortcuts

methods.name: setChoices
methods.rawDocumentation:
Set the choices list. Pass a static array or a function:

- **Array** — the chooser filters it automatically as the user types.
- **Function** — called with the current query string on every `refreshChoices()` and
  on show. The function is responsible for filtering; the chooser displays all items it returns.

- Parameter choices: {Array<Record<string, any>> | ((query: string) => Array<Record<string, any>>)} An array of choice objects, or a function `(query) => [...]`
- Returns: Self for chaining
- Example:
```js
// Static
chooser.setChoices([{text: "Foo"}, {text: "Bar"}])

// Dynamic
chooser.setChoices(q => items.filter(i => i.text.toLowerCase().includes(q.toLowerCase())))
```
methods.description: on show. The function is responsible for filtering; the chooser displays all items it returns.

params.name: choices
params.description: An array of choice objects, or a function `(query) => [...]`

methods.name: refreshChoices
methods.rawDocumentation:
Re-apply filtering (static choices) or re-invoke the choices function (dynamic).
Call after updating an external data source in an async `onQueryChange` handler.
- Returns: Self for chaining
- Example:
```js
chooser.refreshChoices()
```
methods.description:
Re-apply filtering (static choices) or re-invoke the choices function (dynamic).
Call after updating an external data source in an async `onQueryChange` handler.

methods.name: show
methods.rawDocumentation:
Show the chooser.
- Returns: Self for chaining
- Example:
```js
chooser.show()
```
methods.description: Show the chooser.

methods.name: hide
methods.rawDocumentation:
Hide the chooser without making a selection. Restores focus to the previously active window.
- Returns: Self for chaining
- Example:
```js
chooser.hide()
```
methods.description: Hide the chooser without making a selection. Restores focus to the previously active window.

methods.name: select
methods.rawDocumentation:
Programmatically confirm a selection.

Omit `row` to confirm the currently highlighted row. Fires `onSelect` (or `onInvalid`
for rows with `valid: false`) and hides the chooser.

- Parameter row: {number | null} Zero-based row index, or omit to use the current selection.
- Returns: Self for chaining
- Example:
```js
chooser.select()     // confirm highlighted row
chooser.select(2)    // confirm row at index 2
```
methods.description:
Programmatically confirm a selection.
Omit `row` to confirm the currently highlighted row. Fires `onSelect` (or `onInvalid`
for rows with `valid: false`) and hides the chooser.

params.name: row
params.description: Zero-based row index, or omit to use the current selection.

examples.code:
chooser.select()     // confirm highlighted row
chooser.select(2)    // confirm row at index 2

methods.name: selectedRowContents
methods.rawDocumentation:
Returns the dict for the highlighted row, or for a specific row by index.
Returns `null` if the index is out of range or no choices are set.

- Parameter row: {number | null} Zero-based row index, or omit to query the highlighted row.
- Returns: The row dict (`{ text, subText?, image?, valid, ...extras }`) or `null`.
- Example:
```js
const item = chooser.selectedRowContents()
const item = chooser.selectedRowContents(2)
```
methods.description:
Returns the dict for the highlighted row, or for a specific row by index.
Returns `null` if the index is out of range or no choices are set.

params.name: row
params.description: Zero-based row index, or omit to query the highlighted row.

returns.description: The row dict (`{ text, subText?, image?, valid, ...extras }`) or `null`.

examples.code:
const item = chooser.selectedRowContents()
const item = chooser.selectedRowContents(2)

properties.name: typeName
properties.rawDocumentation: Read-only type identifier.
properties.description: Read-only type identifier.

properties.name: identifier
properties.rawDocumentation: Stable UUID string for this chooser instance.
properties.description: Stable UUID string for this chooser instance.

properties.name: query
properties.rawDocumentation:
The current text in the search field. Setting this from JS updates the display but
does not invoke the `onQueryChange` callback.
- Example:
```js
chooser.query = "he"
```
properties.description:
The current text in the search field. Setting this from JS updates the display but
does not invoke the `onQueryChange` callback.

properties.name: placeholder
properties.rawDocumentation:
Placeholder text shown in the empty search field (default: `"Search..."`).
- Example:
```js
chooser.placeholder = "Type to search..."
```
properties.description: Placeholder text shown in the empty search field (default: `"Search..."`).

properties.name: searchSubText
properties.rawDocumentation:
Whether searches match against `subText` in addition to `text` (default: `false`).
Only applies when a static choices array is provided.
- Example:
```js
chooser.searchSubText = true
```
properties.description:
Whether searches match against `subText` in addition to `text` (default: `false`).
Only applies when a static choices array is provided.

properties.name: enableDefaultForQuery
properties.rawDocumentation:
When `true` and the query is non-empty but there are no matching choices, `onSelect`
is called with `{ text: <query> }` instead of `null` (default: `false`).
- Example:
```js
chooser.enableDefaultForQuery = true
```
properties.description:
When `true` and the query is non-empty but there are no matching choices, `onSelect`
is called with `{ text: <query> }` instead of `null` (default: `false`).

properties.name: selectedRow
properties.rawDocumentation:
The zero-based index of the currently highlighted row (-1 when empty).
- Example:
```js
chooser.selectedRow = 0
```
properties.description: The zero-based index of the currently highlighted row (-1 when empty).

properties.name: width
properties.rawDocumentation:
Width of the chooser as a fraction of the screen width (default: `0.5` = 50 %).
- Example:
```js
chooser.width = 0.4
```
properties.description: Width of the chooser as a fraction of the screen width (default: `0.5` = 50 %).

properties.name: visibleRows
properties.rawDocumentation:
Maximum number of rows visible at once without scrolling (default: `10`).
- Example:
```js
chooser.visibleRows = 8
```
properties.description: Maximum number of rows visible at once without scrolling (default: `10`).

properties.name: isVisible
properties.rawDocumentation:
`true` if the chooser panel is currently on screen.
- Example:
```js
if (!chooser.isVisible) chooser.show()
```
properties.description: `true` if the chooser panel is currently on screen.

properties.name: onSelect
properties.description:
Called when the user confirms a selection, or null to remove the handler.
The argument is the chosen row object (the original dict you passed to `setChoices`,
with `text`, `subText`, `image`, `valid`, and any custom fields intact).
The argument is `null` when dismissed (Escape).

properties.name: onQueryChange
properties.rawDocumentation:
Called on every keystroke with the new query string, or null to remove the handler.

Use this to debounce expensive searches or trigger async data fetching.

- Example:
```js
chooser.onQueryChange = (q) => {
    hs.timer.doAfter(0.05, () => { fetchResults(q).then(r => { cache = r; chooser.refreshChoices() }) })
}
```
properties.description:
Called on every keystroke with the new query string, or null to remove the handler.
Use this to debounce expensive searches or trigger async data fetching.

properties.name: onShow
properties.rawDocumentation:
Called after the panel becomes visible, or null to remove the handler.
- Example:
```js
chooser.onShow = () => console.log("Chooser appeared")
```
properties.description: Called after the panel becomes visible, or null to remove the handler.

properties.name: onHide
properties.rawDocumentation:
Called after the panel is hidden (for any reason: selection, Escape, or `hide()`), or null to remove the handler.
- Example:
```js
chooser.onHide = () => console.log("Chooser hidden")
```
properties.description: Called after the panel is hidden (for any reason: selection, Escape, or `hide()`), or null to remove the handler.

properties.name: onInvalid
properties.rawDocumentation:
Called when the user activates a row whose `valid` field is `false`, or null to remove the handler.
The chooser stays open; the argument is the row dict (same shape as `onSelect`).
If unset, activating an invalid row is silently ignored.
- Example:
```js
chooser.onInvalid = (item) => console.log("Tapped invalid row: " + item.text)
```
properties.description:
Called when the user activates a row whose `valid` field is `false`, or null to remove the handler.
The chooser stays open; the argument is the row dict (same shape as `onSelect`).
If unset, activating an invalid row is silently ignored.

methods.name: aesGcmEncryptB64
methods.rawDocumentation:
AES-256-GCM encrypt. Inputs and outputs are base64 strings.
- Parameter opts: `{ keyB64: string, nonceB64: string, plaintext: string }`
  - `keyB64`: base64-encoded 32-byte key
  - `nonceB64`: base64-encoded 12-byte nonce (reuse-once invariant on caller)
  - `plaintext`: UTF-8 string to encrypt
- Returns: `{ nonceB64, ciphertextB64 }` where ciphertextB64 includes the 16-byte tag at the end
- Example:
```js
const { ciphertextB64 } = hs.crypto.aesGcmEncryptB64({ keyB64, nonceB64, plaintext: 'hi' })
```

methods.name: aesGcmDecryptB64
methods.rawDocumentation:
AES-256-GCM authenticate-and-decrypt. Returns the plaintext UTF-8 string,
or null on auth failure / bad input. Designed to round-trip with Android's
Cipher.getInstance("AES/GCM/NoPadding") output (ciphertext+tag concatenated).
- Parameter opts: `{ keyB64: string, nonceB64: string, ciphertextB64: string }`
- Returns: the decoded UTF-8 plaintext, or null if authentication failed
- Example:
```js
const plain = hs.crypto.aesGcmDecryptB64({ keyB64, nonceB64, ciphertextB64 })
if (plain === null) { /* auth failed */ }
```

modules.description:
# hs.docs
**Offline API documentation browser**
Browse and query the Hammerspoon 2 API documentation from within the app.
`hs.docs.show()` opens an `hs.ui.webview` window with the JS or TypeScript docs.
`hs.docs.get()` returns formatted plain-text documentation from the bundled `api.json`.
modules.rawDocumentation:
# hs.docs

**Offline API documentation browser**

Browse and query the Hammerspoon 2 API documentation from within the app.

`hs.docs.show()` opens an `hs.ui.webview` window with the JS or TypeScript docs.
`hs.docs.get()` returns formatted plain-text documentation from the bundled `api.json`.

modules.name: hs.eventtap

methods.name: makeTap
methods.signature: func makeTap(_ eventTypes: [String], _ callback: JSValue) -> HSEventTap
methods.rawDocumentation:
Create a new event tap for the specified event types.
Call .start() on the returned object to begin receiving events.
Requires Accessibility permission (active event taps; keyboard monitoring may also need Input Monitoring).
- Parameter eventTypes: Array of event type strings: 'keyDown', 'keyUp', 'flagsChanged', 'mouseMoved', 'leftMouseDown', 'leftMouseUp', 'rightMouseDown', 'rightMouseUp', 'otherMouseDown', 'otherMouseUp', 'leftMouseDragged', 'rightMouseDragged', 'scrollWheel', 'systemDefined', 'gesture', 'magnify'.
  'systemDefined' delivers media-key events (brightness, volume, play/pause…): `{type, subtype, modifiers}` plus — for subtype 8 (aux control buttons) — `key` (e.g. 'BRIGHTNESS_UP'), `nxKeyCode`, `down`, `isRepeat`.
  'gesture' delivers raw trackpad touch frames: `{type, modifiers, touchCount, touches: [{id, phase, x, y}]}` with x/y normalized to the pad (origin bottom-left).
  'magnify' delivers trackpad pinch-zoom gestures: `{type, modifiers, magnification, phase}` where `magnification` is the incremental scale delta for the frame (positive = zoom in) and `phase` is 'began' | 'changed' | 'ended' | 'cancelled'.
- Parameter callback: Function called with an event object. Return true to consume (suppress) the event.
- Returns: An HSEventTap instance
- Example:
```js
const tap = hs.eventtap.make(['keyDown'], e => {
  console.log(e.keyCode, e.modifiers)
  return false // don't consume
})
tap.start()

// Watch media keys (volume/brightness):
const media = hs.eventtap.make(['systemDefined'], e => {
  if (e.subtype === 8 && e.down) console.log(e.key)
  return false
})
media.start()
```
methods.description:
Create a new event tap for the specified event types.
Call .start() on the returned object to begin receiving events.
Requires Accessibility permission (active event taps; keyboard monitoring may also need Input Monitoring).
'systemDefined' delivers media-key events (brightness, volume, play/pause…): `{type, subtype, modifiers}` plus — for subtype 8 (aux control buttons) — `key` (e.g. 'BRIGHTNESS_UP'), `nxKeyCode`, `down`, `isRepeat`.
'gesture' delivers raw trackpad touch frames: `{type, modifiers, touchCount, touches: [{id, phase, x, y}]}` with x/y normalized to the pad (origin bottom-left).
'magnify' delivers trackpad pinch-zoom gestures: `{type, modifiers, magnification, phase}` where `magnification` is the incremental scale delta for the frame (positive = zoom in) and `phase` is 'began' | 'changed' | 'ended' | 'cancelled'.

params.name: eventTypes
params.description: Array of event type strings: 'keyDown', 'keyUp', 'flagsChanged', 'mouseMoved', 'leftMouseDown', 'leftMouseUp', 'rightMouseDown', 'rightMouseUp', 'otherMouseDown', 'otherMouseUp', 'leftMouseDragged', 'rightMouseDragged', 'scrollWheel', 'systemDefined', 'gesture', 'magnify'.

params.name: callback
params.description: Function called with an event object. Return true to consume (suppress) the event.

methods.name: keyStroke
methods.signature: func keyStroke(_ mods: [String], _ key: String, _ delay: JSValue)
methods.rawDocumentation:
Synthesise a key stroke: press the modifiers + key, hold, then release.
- Parameter mods: Array of modifier strings, e.g. ['cmd']
- Parameter key: Key name, e.g. 'v'
- Parameter delay: Optional number of microseconds the key is held between keyDown
  and keyUp. Defaults to 200000 (200 ms), matching upstream Hammerspoon's
  `hs.eventtap.keyStroke`. A zero/too-short hold is frequently dropped by the target
  app — the clipboard gets set but the paste never lands.
- Example:
```js
hs.eventtap.keyStroke(['cmd'], 'v')          // 200 ms default hold
hs.eventtap.keyStroke(['cmd'], 'v', 10000)   // 10 ms hold
```
methods.description:
Synthesise a key stroke: press the modifiers + key, hold, then release.
and keyUp. Defaults to 200000 (200 ms), matching upstream Hammerspoon's
`hs.eventtap.keyStroke`. A zero/too-short hold is frequently dropped by the target
app — the clipboard gets set but the paste never lands.

params.name: mods
params.description: Array of modifier strings, e.g. ['cmd']

params.name: key
params.description: Key name, e.g. 'v'

params.name: delay
params.description: Optional number of microseconds the key is held between keyDown

examples.code:
hs.eventtap.keyStroke(['cmd'], 'v')          // 200 ms default hold
hs.eventtap.keyStroke(['cmd'], 'v', 10000)   // 10 ms hold

methods.name: scrollWheel
methods.signature: func scrollWheel(_ dx: Double, _ dy: Double, _ unit: JSValue)
methods.rawDocumentation:
Synthesise a scroll-wheel event at the current mouse position.
The window server routes scroll events to the window under the cursor —
independent of keyboard focus — so this scrolls whatever the mouse is over.
- Parameter dx: Horizontal scroll amount (positive scrolls left, like a physical wheel tilt-left)
- Parameter dy: Vertical scroll amount (positive scrolls up, like a physical wheel-up)
- Parameter unit: Optional unit string: 'pixel' (default) or 'line'
- Example:
```js
hs.eventtap.scrollWheel(0, -450, 'pixel')  // scroll down 450px under the cursor
hs.eventtap.scrollWheel(0, 3, 'line')      // scroll up 3 lines
```
methods.description:
Synthesise a scroll-wheel event at the current mouse position.
The window server routes scroll events to the window under the cursor —
independent of keyboard focus — so this scrolls whatever the mouse is over.

params.name: dy
params.description: Vertical scroll amount (positive scrolls up, like a physical wheel-up)
